claim: GStack implements the 'thin harness, fat skills' pattern where a minimal orchestration layer coordinates multiple specialized AI agents with different roles (code review, design, QA, etc.)
actionable: Structure your AI systems as a thin controller + fat specialized skills; this scales better than one monolithic agent
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Your AI Engineering Team

claim: Office Hours skill pressure-tests product ideas before coding by asking forcing questions that reframe the problem and surface overlooked assumptions
actionable: Before implementing, use an AI agent to simulate YC-style office hours: ask hard questions about user pain, market size, competitive differentiation
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Your AI Engineering Team

claim: Adversarial review skill automatically identifies risks and gaps in design docs (auth, privacy, failure handling, 2FA), catches and fixes issues autonomously
actionable: Apply adversarial review to design specs before engineering: have an AI agent systematically find security, privacy, and reliability gaps
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Your AI Engineering Team

claim: GStack enables running 10-15+ parallel Claude Code sessions on independent features simultaneously with automated QA, landing 10-50 PRs per day
actionable: Scale AI coding through parallelization: run multiple agents on different features in parallel branches, merge with automated QA
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Your AI Engineering Team

claim: The bottleneck in AI coding is not model intelligence but system scaffolding; with proper context and processes, models are already capable of extraordinary work
actionable: Focus engineering effort on context management and workflow orchestration, not on waiting for better models
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Your AI Engineering Team

claim: GStack wraps Playwright CLI for browser automation because Claude in Chrome MCP was slow, context-heavy, and unreliable (2-3 sec per action, often failed)
actionable: For critical browser interactions, implement custom wrappers around headless browsers (Playwright, Puppeteer) rather than relying on slow MCP tools
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Your AI Engineering Team

claim: Opus 4.6 is described as 'ADHD CEO' (many ideas) and Codex as 'autistic CTO' (deep focus); selecting the right model for the right role matters
actionable: Use Opus for brainstorming and planning, switch to Codex for detailed, focused problem-solving
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Your AI Engineering Team

claim: GStack has acquired more GitHub stars than Ruby on Rails within weeks of launch, indicating rapid community adoption of agentic coding patterns
actionable: Monitor GitHub stars and community adoption as signals of framework-level impact; rapidly rising adoption indicates product-market fit
source_title: How to Make Claude Code Your AI Engineering Team
